_meta.name: CLM 3B r1 initial eval — PIPELINE VALIDATION (NOT SOTA)
_meta.purpose: Validate training pipeline at 3B scale; corpus 45x undertrained per Chinchilla so not a quality attempt

label: PIPELINE_VALIDATION

feasibility_math_A.verdict: FITS — batch=8 successfully running at 50.6GB steady (63% util)

corpus_adequacy_B.flag_resolution: User pre-approved as pipeline-validation only; proceed with explicit label
corpus_adequacy_B.verdict: UNDERFIT-EXPECTED — 45x Chinchilla undertraining is well beyond 30x flag. Model will plateau early. PIPELINE VALIDATION ONLY, do not treat as a quality or SOTA attempt.

next_action: CONTINUE MONITORING — pod healthy, loss trending down (1.74 at step 1000 from initial ~5.5), VRAM stable. First eval at step 500 and 1000 both completed without abort. Let training run to next eval checkpoints; next cron/loop tick should confirm step 2000 eval + first ckpt save at step 2000 for MFS quota test.
notes: Loss at 1.74 is already similar to CLM 1B r3f early eval — scale provides faster convergence early but 45x undertraining will cap improvement
VRAM headroom suggests batch=16 would also fit (~65GB est) but leaving batch=8 to match config template
Phi violation #1 at step 1000 (drop 586.6 -> 474.7) is informational; 1B r3f had 8 violations and still reached best eval 1.23
First ckpt save at step 2000 is the real MFS quota stress test — 3B ckpt ~18GB model + ~25GB optim = ~43GB single save
IMPORTANT: corpus 45x undertrained means best_eval will plateau early (est 1.3-1.5 range). Do not compare to 1B r3f eval 1.23 as that had sufficient relative data
